# JLWS conformance report — solid-server-rs feat/lws @ 0f2082c (EXPERIMENTAL, in-memory, LWS surface + strict PUT, authenticated controller)

| | |
|---|---|
| Target | `http://127.0.0.1:3000/alice` |
| Generated | 2026-07-06T17:37:49.075Z |
| Harness | jlws-conformance-suite 0.1.0 |
| Statement companions (spec pin) | core `c933aee98f95`, rdf-transform `c933aee98f95` |
| Vector suite pin | `lws-spec@f2e081d` (150 cases) |
| Target features/capabilities declared | ContentNegotiation |
| Agent credential seams configured | 0 |

> EXPERIMENTAL-SERVER RUN: the target is solid-server-rs — the EXPERIMENTAL Rust Solid server (NOT the production TypeScript prod-solid-server; do not deploy it) — branch feat/lws @ 0f2082c, booted locally with its opt-in JLWS surface ON (SOLID_SERVER_LWS=1, strict D2/D3 PUT via SOLID_SERVER_LWS_STRICT_PUT=1, RDF transform on), in-memory store, seeded pods (SOLID_SERVER_SEED_CONFORMANCE=1), and the LWS RFC 9068 at+jwt Bearer chain against a local mock AS. The server's WAC is fail-closed (no ACL anywhere = deny), so the harness authenticates its storage-controller requests as the seeded pod owner (--controller-bearer) and --target points inside the seeded pod. Unlike the CSS baseline, this target CLAIMS JLWS conformance — its storage description advertises conformsTo core/1.0 + transform/rdf-1 — so executed failures are genuine findings against a claimed surface: implementation bugs (container-listing media-type discipline, POST typed-link container creation, linkset merge-patch update semantics, expanded-array JSON-LD transform output, write-time RDF validation vs authoritative-bytes) plus one not-yet-implemented core obligation (RFC 9457 problem details on the core 4xx path). The top findings are filed as issues on jeswr/solid-server-rs. No vector was weakened.

## How to read this scoreboard

- The report is keyed to the **normative-statement companion IDs** (`index.statements.ttl`, `rdf-transform.statements.ttl`).
- Only **server-deniable, enforceable (E)** statements execute, through their wired `http-exchange` vectors.
- **Audit-class (A-int / A-exist)** statements are listed as evidence-checks — an implementation satisfies them by review/attestation, never by a vector.
- **Premature (P)** statements are explicitly untestable today.
- `untested (…)` rows are honest non-execution, each with the reason; they are **not** passes.
- SHOULD/MAY-level failures are reportable, not disqualifying (W3C practice).

## Evidence-checks — audit-class statements (A-int / A-exist) (14)

| Statement | Level | Subject(s) | Detail |
|---|---|---|---|
| JLWSC-CP-6 | MAY | Server | Operative sub-clause here is the constrained MAY: how an approximation is computed is internal (a caller-visible-derived approximation is indistinguishable black-box from a leaky one), so accountability only. |
| JLWSC-CTN-5 | MUST | Server | Universal state invariant ('no orphans'): spot-checkable per resource but not enumerable black-box over all server behaviour; audit-level. |
| JLWSC-CTN-6 | MUST NOT | Server | State invariant ('no cycles'); a move-supporting server exposes a rejectable probe (move a container into its own descendant), otherwise audit-level. |
| JLWSC-GE-1 | SHOULD | Server | Behavioural emission (GAPS.md): what a server publishes as a side effect, auditable rather than vectorable. |
| JLWSC-GR-2 | MUST | Server | Operative sub-clause here is the bounded-documented-interval MUST: the bound is a documented artifact (audit), the reflection temporal. |
| JLWSC-GR-3 | SHOULD | Server | Operative sub-clause here is the SHOULD-be-immediate recommendation. |
| JLWSC-MD-2 | MUST | Server | Concurrency atomicity: exercisable only by nondeterministic race probing, not deterministic vectors. |
| JLWSC-POST-6 | MUST | Server | Concurrency atomicity; the sequential effects are pinned by the create vectors, the atomicity itself is not black-box observable. |
| JLWSC-PRIV-4 | SHOULD NOT | Server, Client, AuthorizationServer | audit-class statement (accountable, not enforceable black-box): satisfy by implementation evidence/attestation, not by vector execution |
| JLWSC-SECT-1 | SHOULD | Server | Deployment-posture recommendation; auditable, not vectorable (whether data is 'high-value' is a policy judgement). |
| JLWSC-ST-3 | MUST NOT | Server, Client, AuthorizationServer | Negative internal property (audit/code-review only). Capability URLs are the deliberate, constrained exception (single-purpose, expiring, revocable, TLS-only — JLWSC-SSE-2). |
| JLWSC-WS-1 | SHOULD | NotificationServer | There is no replay on this binding; clients needing gapless delivery use SSE or webhooks. |
| JLWSR-CAP-1 | MUST | RdfTransformingServer | Advertisement duty: an auditor can check the storage description contains the entries, but an unadvertised-yet-supported source type is not externally observable and no rejection path is mandated. |
| JLWSR-PATCH-4 | SHOULD | RdfTransformingServer | The recommended artifact (the normalizes flag on the patched type's entry) is auditable in the storage description; whether 'this matters' held for a given server is not. |
